# CS-Pedia
> CS conference information platform for Korean CS researchers

CS-Pedia (cs-pedia.io) is a comprehensive platform that tracks 144 CS conferences with deadline information, institutional recognition ratings (BK21, KIISE, KAIST, SNU, POSTECH), best paper awards (1400+ papers from 2020-2026), and acceptance rate trends.

## Key Data

- 144 CS conferences across all major fields (AI/ML, Systems, Theory, HCI, Security, etc.)
- Conference deadlines with D-day countdown (176 deadline entries)
- Institutional recognition: BK21 (Korean government), KIISE (Korean CS society), KAIST/SNU/POSTECH tier ratings
- Best paper awards archive: 1400+ papers from 2020-2026 with tags and paper links
- Acceptance rate trends from DBLP and OpenAlex
- Keyword trends from Semantic Scholar paper titles

## Pages

- / : Conference listing with filters by field, institution recognition, and search
- /conferences/[slug] : Individual conference detail (deadlines, acceptance rates, best papers, keyword trends)
- /best-papers : Best paper awards archive with filtering by conference, year, and award type
- /trends : Acceptance rate and keyword trend visualizations

## Data Sources

- DBLP (CC0) - publication counts and acceptance rates
- OpenAlex (CC0) - acceptance rate data
- jeffhuang.com - best paper awards data
- Semantic Scholar - keyword trend analysis
- NRF Korea (한국연구재단) - BK21 recognized conference list
- KIISE (한국정보과학회) - KIISE recognized conference list

## Contact

contact@cs-pedia.io
